factors.summary: Demand is decelerating due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: Operating margin was negatively impacted by increased restructuring and realignment costs of $22 million and special charges of $11 million incurred in during the quarter. Excluding these restructuring and realignment costs and special charges, adjusted operating income was $108 million with an adjusted operating margin of 9.3% in the second quarter of 2020 as compared to adjusted operating income of $192 million with an adjusted operating margin of 14.3% in the second quarter of 2019. The decrease in adjusted operating margin was primarily due to unfavorable volume, impacted significantly by COVID-19; cost inflation; increased inventory management costs; incremental COVID-19 related costs; increased spending on strategic investments and unfavorable mix.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Revenue growth is driven by real demand decline due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: The decrease in adjusted operating margin was primarily due to unfavorable volume, impacted significantly by COVID-19; cost inflation; increased inventory management costs; unfavorable mix; increased spending on strategic investments and other lesser impacts. These impacts were partially offset by cost reductions from our productivity and other cost saving initiatives and price realization.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Revenue growth is driven by real demand decline due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, showing a negative trend.

factors.summary: Cost inflation is creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: The decrease in adjusted operating margin was primarily due to unfavorable volume, impacted significantly by COVID-19; cost inflation; increased inventory management costs; unfavorable mix; increased spending on strategic investments and other lesser impacts. These impacts were partially offset by cost reductions from our productivity and other cost saving initiatives and price realization.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Cost inflation is creating margin pressure, which is a significant concern for profitability.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to various factors including cost inflation and unfavorable volume.

evidence.text: Operating margin was negatively impacted by increased restructuring and realignment costs of $22 million and special charges of $11 million incurred in during the quarter. Excluding these restructuring and realignment costs and special charges, adjusted operating income was $108 million with an adjusted operating margin of 9.3% in the second quarter of 2020 as compared to adjusted operating income of $192 million with an adjusted operating margin of 14.3% in the second quarter of 2019. The decrease in adjusted operating margin was primarily due to unfavorable volume, impacted significantly by COVID-19; cost inflation; increased inventory management costs; incremental COVID-19 related costs; increased spending on strategic investments and unfavorable mix.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to various factors including cost inflation and unfavorable volume, indicating a negative trend.

factors.summary: Restructuring programs are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: We currently expect to incur between $60 million and $80 million in restructuring costs for the full year, with approximately $20 million of these costs being non-cash charges. These restructuring charges are primarily related to actions taken in response to the changes in business and economic conditions arising as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic. We expect to realize approximately $25 million of incremental net savings in 2020 from restructuring actions initiated in 2019, and approximately $22 million of net savings in 2020 from the restructuring actions initiated during this year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring programs are delivering measurable savings, which is a positive development for cost management.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include unfavorable volume, cost inflation, and unfavorable mix.

evidence.text: The decrease in adjusted operating margin was primarily due to unfavorable volume, impacted significantly by COVID-19; cost inflation; increased inventory management costs; unfavorable mix; increased spending on strategic investments and other lesser impacts. These impacts were partially offset by cost reductions from our productivity and other cost saving initiatives and price realization.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include unfavorable volume, cost inflation, and unfavorable mix, which are significant concerns.

factors.summary: R&D spending is decreasing as a percentage of revenue.

evidence.text: R&D expense was $44 million, or 3.8% of revenue, in the second quarter of 2020, as compared to $47 million, or 3.5% of revenue, in the comparable period of 2019; and was $93 million, or 4.1% of revenue, in the six months ended June 30, 2020, as compared to $98 million, or 3.8% of revenue, in the comparable period of 2019. The increase in R&D as a percent of revenue for both periods was primarily driven by the drop in revenue, which was significantly driven by impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: R&D spending is decreasing as a percentage of revenue, which may indicate reduced investment in innovation, a negative trend.

factors.summary: The economic environment is a headwind due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: The decrease in adjusted operating margin was primarily due to unfavorable volume, impacted significantly by COVID-19; cost inflation; increased inventory management costs; unfavorable mix; increased spending on strategic investments and other lesser impacts. These impacts were partially offset by cost reductions from our productivity and other cost saving initiatives and price realization.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The economic environment is a headwind due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, creating unfavorable conditions for growth.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are creating both favorable and unfavorable impacts.

evidence.text: Other comprehensive income was $52 million for the three months ended June 30, 2020 compared to a loss of $11 million for the same period in 2019. Foreign currency translation contributed favorable impacts during the quarter of $53 million, driven primarily by the strengthening of the British Pound, the Australian Dollar and the Chinese Yuan as compared to the U.S. Dollar in 2020 versus the weakening of these currencies in the same prior year period. Additionally, the strengthening of the Euro as compared to the U.S. Dollar was greater in 2020 than the strengthening in the prior year. These favorable currency translation impacts were partially offset by the movement in our Euro net investment hedges during the quarter.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The factor shows both favorable and unfavorable impacts from foreign exchange movements, resulting in a mixed sentiment.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving due to higher financing activities.

evidence.text: Cash generated by financing activities was $774 million for the six months ended June 30, 2020 as compared to cash generated of $25 million in the comparable prior year period. This increase in cash generated by financing activities during the period was primarily driven by the issuance of our Green Bond and higher levels of short-term debt during the year, partially offset by the repayment of short-term debt during 2020 and an increase in share repurchase activity of $21 million.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow is improving due to higher financing activities, indicating a positive trend.
